_comment_2_: Relative inflow path is resolved against this JSON's parent directory,
_comment_3_: so the example runs from any working directory:
_comment_6_: Window is dateless: 'window_start' / 'window_end' are public names defined in
_comment_6b_: inflow.py, evaluated at sidecar-import time (a few minutes wide) -- no env vars needed.
_comment_7_: 'nav' discovers 150 HATEOAS urls; 'pokemon' is a Stream(parent_current='nav') T5 drill
_comment_8_: against each url's real /pokemon/{id}/ payload -- this is where base_stat_total becomes
_comment_9_: real (not the shallow list, which has no per-Pokemon stats). 'export_pokemon' is required:
_comment_10_: Stream(parent_current=...)'s own export_params is never consumed by the scheduler, so a
_comment_11_: separate Export current is the only thing that writes the drilled rows to disk.
_comment_12_: 'nav' fetches limit=150 in ONE call (not 3 paginated limit=50 pages) and sets inc_code
_comment_13_: so a Stream current's implicit refresh-per-chunk upserts in place -- see inflow.py's
